_comment: Living symbol table for build/OREGON_unpacked.exe. seg:off are relative segment values as stored in the relocated image. Confidence: high=verified by strings/use, med=strong inference, low=guess. Grows as decompilation proceeds.

20a4:0000.name: cstartup
20a4:0000.note: first C runtime init call from main

20a4:00d8.name: _exit
20a4:00d8.note: called with code in AX at program end

20a4:0244.name: __stkcheck
20a4:0244.note: Borland stack-overflow check; called at every function prologue with frame size in AX

20a4:0634.name: far_sprintf
20a4:0634.note: format into ss:buf from cs:fmt + args

2042:0000.name: init_input
2042:0000.note: boot: init the keyboard/input module (int 16h); segment 0x2042

2042:0215.name: show_input_prompt
2042:0215.note: show the input cursor/prompt before a read

1ceb:1357.name: init_graphics
1ceb:1357.note: boot: bring up graphics via BGI driver (VGA256.BGI/CGA.BGI), int 10h

1049:1855.name: draw_text
1049:1855.note: render '\'-separated multi-line string at (x,y), 9px line spacing

1049:0ebd.name: print_string
1049:0ebd.note: draw string at cursor, advance X by 8px/char

1049:0057.name: strlen
1049:0057.note: used by print_string to advance cursor

150c:301f.name: draw_string
150c:301f.note: draw counted string with alignment; width calc + per-glyph loop; font from BIT8X8.GFT

150c:164d.name: draw_glyph
150c:164d.note: draw one char: width=font+0x44 tab, bitmap=font+0x4c+font+0x48 tab; blit via vtable jmp far [drv+0x24]

150c:019d.name: font_lookup
150c:019d.note: resolve a font id to its descriptor (far ptr in dx:bx)

150c:0ea4.name: font_data_locate
150c:0ea4.note: locate glyph data for a font

150c:191e.name: blit_glyph_cga
150c:191e.note: CGA glyph blitter (B800, 0x2000/0xc050 interleave): row-major, height rows x ceil(w/8) bytes, bit->fg/bg via xlat

d08:2217.name: travel_the_trail
d08:2217.note: menu option 1 - sets up flags + fresh game state

d08:20ee.name: prompt_continue_saved_game
d08:20ee.note: 'Would you like to continue a saved game?' - load/select/delete save slot

d08:1f7c.name: start_new_game
d08:1f7c.note: run 4 setup screens, then init game state

1049:0d95.name: read_field
1049:0d95.note: read text field (charset-filtered, counted-string result); Ctrl-S(0x13) toggles sound, Esc=quit

1049:0c12.name: read_input
1049:0c12.note: core char-reader: echo + charset filter into counted string

1049:0e17.name: counted_str_width
1049:0e17.note: pixel width of a counted string (len*8)

d08:04d0.name: name_party
d08:04d0.note: leader (required) + 4 members; blanks get random default; 11-byte name records at 0x1589

d08:032e.name: read_member_name
d08:032e.note: near; read one party member name into slot

d08:1e78.name: buy_supplies
d08:1e78.note: Independence store: show supplies.pcc, run buy loop, confirm departure

d08:1938.name: store_buy_loop
d08:1938.note: Matt's General Store: 5 categories (Oxen/Food/Clothing/Ammunition/Spare parts), total bill vs cash, buy/leave loop

1049:2ccc.name: format_money
1049:2ccc.note: format a long as currency string

20a4:025d.name: memcpy_n
20a4:025d.note: copy n bytes (n in stack arg, here 143)

c6b:051b.name: learn_about_trail
c6b:051b.note: menu option 2 - 8-page info slideshow incl. credits

c00:0433.name: see_top_ten
c00:0433.note: menu 3: framed Top Ten screen, then offers scoring explanation

f34:0fad.name: choose_management
f34:0fad.note: menu 5: view/erase Top Ten, erase tombstones/saves, joystick on/off/calibrate

1049:2a47.name: show_top_ten
1049:2a47.note: render a Top Ten list table

0032:3f93.name: travel_loop
0032:3f93.note: MAIN TRAIL LOOP: iterate 17 locations; status/turn/landmark/death; seg 0x0032 = trail engine

0032:3cb3.name: travel_turn
0032:3cb3.note: per-turn status panel + action menu dispatch (Continue/Check/Map/Pace/Rations/Rest/Trade/Hunt or Talk/Buy)

0032:3b68.name: must_trade_msg
0032:3b68.note: blocked: 'You must trade for <item> to be able to continue.' (g_must_trade set)

0032:361a.name: continue_travel
0032:361a.note: advance to next location: fort check, river crossing, distance 'From X it is N miles to Y.'

0442:18bb.name: cross_river
0442:18bb.note: river crossing: show width/depth, offer ford/caulk/ferry/indian/wait/info, dispatch

0442:02be.name: ford_river
0442:02be.note: ford: random outcome by depth - success/muddy/rough/tipped over/too deep (lose supplies/oxen/people)

0442:0695.name: caulk_and_float
0442:0695.note: crossing method: caulk wagon and float

0442:0e2c.name: hire_indian
0442:0e2c.note: crossing method: hire an Indian to help (1990 wording)

0442:1090.name: wait_conditions
0442:1090.note: wait to see if river conditions improve

0442:126f.name: get_more_info
0442:126f.note: get more information about the river

0032:2dbe.name: trail_fork
0032:2dbe.note: 'The trail divides here. You may: 1. head for.. 2... 3. see the map'

0032:11da.name: event_fire
0032:11da.note: random event: 'A fire in the wagon results in the loss of:'

0032:23fb.name: event_thief
0032:23fb.note: random event: 'A thief comes during the night and steals'

1049:39b9.name: pick_member
1049:39b9.note: choose a party member for an event

1049:3910.name: draw_msg_box
1049:3910.note: draw a horizontally-centered message box (measures text width, centers at x=160)

1049:36de.name: apply_member_illness
1049:36de.note: apply an illness/injury to a party member

1049:3be4.name: daily_update
1049:3be4.note: per-day sim: advance calendar, roll member illness, recompute party health

0032:2d16.name: look_at_map
0032:2d16.note: menu 3: show MAP.PCX trail map with current position marked

182e:1e2d.name: load_show_pcx
182e:1e2d.note: load and display a standalone .PCX (graphics module)

0032:2ca2.name: save_restore_screen
0032:2ca2.note: save/restore the screen behind a temporary display

0032:2ab6.name: draw_map_marker
0032:2ab6.note: mark current position on the map

07ce:2950.name: stop_to_rest
07ce:2950.note: menu 6: rest N days (0-9), advancing the calendar each day until done or death

2042:029e.name: advance_day
2042:029e.note: advance the calendar one day (segment 0x2042 = date/time)

07ce:11c3.name: attempt_trade
07ce:11c3.note: menu 7: random emigrant trade offer ('No one wants to trade...' / 'You meet another emigrant...'); can resolve must-trade; costs a day

07ce:17eb.name: talk_to_people
07ce:17eb.note: menu 8 at forts: read random DIALOGS.REC record, show '<name> tells you: "..."'

20a4:15ba.name: fopen
20a4:15ba.note: Borland C stdio: open a .REC data file

20a4:173b.name: fread_rec
20a4:173b.note: read a record from an open file

0606:1968.name: hunt
0606:1968.note: hunting minigame: terrain/animals.pcc, shoot, meat capped at 100 carry / 2000 wagon

0606:1480.name: hunt_shoot
0606:1480.note: near; real-time aim/shoot action, returns animals shot

14c6:03ea.name: image_blit
14c6:03ea.note: blit a loaded image at x

07ce:1d3c.name: buy_at_fort
07ce:1d3c.note: menu 9 at forts: store with fort pricing, big per-item switch

0032:03a6.name: look_around_prompt
0032:03a6.note: arrival: 'You are now at <X>. Would you like to look around?'

0032:011d.name: show_location
0032:011d.note: draw the current location screen (used at arrival look-around and at the end)

07ce:0f37.name: death_sequence
07ce:0f37.note: tombstone + epitaph (TOMB.REC); 'All of the people in your party have died.'

07ce:0db9.name: show_death_msg
07ce:0db9.note: near; draw the cause-of-death message with screen save/restore

07ce:039a.name: show_ending
07ce:039a.note: endgame: 'made it to Oregon!', tally score (members/supplies/cash x occupation factor), Oregon Top Ten or 'not enough to qualify'

1049:15a0.name: press_any_key
1049:15a0.note: show prompt at bottom, save/restore screen, wait for key

1049:1b3c.name: draw_text_line
1049:1b3c.note: draw single text line at position

1796.name: g_last_key
1796.note: last key code read; 0x13 = Ctrl-S (sound toggle)

1674.name: g_topten_cur
1674.note: current Oregon Top Ten high-score list

06a0.name: g_text_margin_x
06a0.note: left margin x for body text

15c1.name: g_month
15c1.note: current month 1..12 (wraps at 13 -> year++); initialised to departure month (3=Mar..7=Jul), which also drives the score factor

15c0.name: g_day
15c0.note: day counter within the travel period

1570.name: g_choice
1570.note: scratch for last parsed numeric menu input

15d2.name: g_cash
15d2.note: player money (long at 0x15d2); seeded by occupation, shown as 'Amount you have' in the store

1552.name: g_first_turn
1552.note: set at game start, cleared after first travel turn

1729.name: g_at_fort
1729.note: at a fort/settlement: enables Talk (8) and Buy supplies (9)

177c.name: g_must_trade
177c.note: a missing part blocks travel until traded for

15ca.name: g_food
15ca.note: wagon food supply in pounds; max 2000, hunt carry limit 100

1589.name: g_party_names
1589.note: party member names; record 0 = wagon leader
